key_strengths: 30-year EDA duopoly with Synopsys, near-100% customer retention, and extreme switching costs
85%+ gross margins with $1.5B annual FCF at 135% conversion of net income — elite software economics
Record $7.8B backlog locking in ~67% of next year's revenue with 91% recurring revenue mix
Fortress balance sheet: net cash of $451M, 18x interest coverage, could retire all debt in under 2 years
Secular AI tailwind as chip design complexity and custom silicon proliferation expand EDA addressable market
key_risks: Valuation compression: at 50x owner earnings, even modest growth disappointment could trigger 30-40% downside
China export controls tightening: 12-13% of revenue at risk, compounded by $140M BIS/DOJ penalty history
AI commoditization of EDA tools: low-medium probability but high severity if open-source or AI-native design gains traction
Stock-based compensation of $600M+ annually creates meaningful shareholder dilution, flat share count despite buybacks
Semiconductor cyclical downturn could pressure bookings growth over a 5-year horizon
red_flags: $140M BIS/DOJ penalty for illegal exports to Chinese military-linked entities signals past compliance failures
Stock trades 17% above base-case intrinsic value with 2:1 unfavorable downside/upside asymmetry
SBC creates ~16 percentage point gap between GAAP and non-GAAP operating margins
buy_triggers: Stock declines to $220-240 range (~35-40x owner earnings)
Revenue growth accelerates above 15% sustained, demonstrating AI tailwinds stronger than expected
Synopsys-Ansys integration stumbles, creating meaningful share gain opportunity
sell_triggers: Revenue growth decelerates below 8% for two consecutive years without cyclical explanation
Open-source or AI-native EDA tools gain meaningful adoption at major foundries
Management makes a large, value-destructive acquisition departing from disciplined tuck-in M&A track record
Stock reaches 70x+ owner earnings with no growth acceleration
valuation_summary: At ~$293 and ~50x owner earnings ($5.83/share), CDNS is priced for near-perfection. Base-case DCF yields ~$250 (15% overvalued), bear case ~$177 (40% downside), bull case ~$348 (19% upside). Requires 18% annual earnings growth for 5 years to deliver 10% returns. The market is pricing sustained mid-teens growth with premium multiple persistence.
source_summary: Cadence Q4/FY2025 earnings release (Feb 2026), 10-K FY2025, Q3 2025 10-Q, DEF 14A 2024 proxy, Yahoo Finance, StockAnalysis, MacroTrends, GuruFocus, Seeking Alpha, Simply Wall St, Mordor Intelligence EDA market report, BIS.gov, DOJ press releases, PortfoliosLab, MarketBeat, CNBC.
change_notes: Initial full analysis. Exceptional business quality (avg 8.6 on umbrellas 1-5) offset by premium valuation and negative price margin of safety (avg 4.7 on umbrellas 6-8). Watch for price entry at $220-240.
